section.name: Getting Started With BreakTest
section.p: This page covers only BreakTest-specific startup details. For general JMX test-plan authoring, component behavior, and inherited JMeter-compatible concepts, use the official Apache JMeter User Manual.

ul.li: A BreakTest binary release from the download page.

section.p: On Windows, use bin/breaktest.bat. The GUI is intended for recording, scripting, debugging, correlation, and reviewing changes. Use CLI mode for real load generation.
section.h2: Run A Test In CLI Mode
section.p: BreakTest keeps JMeter-compatible command-line options where practical, so existing automation usually needs only the executable name changed.
BreakTest includes command wrappers for controlling a running CLI test through the existing command port:
Pause and Resume are BreakTest additions. In closed-model schedules, pause holds active threads. In open-model schedules, new arrivals pause while active threads finish naturally.
BreakTest includes local agent entry points used by tools such as Codex, Claude Code, and opencode:
These tools allow an AI agent to inspect and repair a JMX script, add correlation and assertions, parameterize dynamic values, and surface changes back in the GUI for review.

The runtime still uses some compatibility names, including jmeter.properties, JMETER_HOME, jmeter.log, ApacheJMeter.jar, and org.apache.jmeter packages. These names are intentionally retained where changing them would break existing plans, plugins, scripts, or tooling.
